description_long: This object consists of two adjacent blue newspaper stands placed on a concrete sidewalk, one slightly taller and narrower than the other. The left stand is boxy with two transparent display windows on the front and side, both featuring the San Jose State University (SJSU) logo and branding. The right stand is more slender, with a prominent 'AUTO VENTA' sign on the side and a vertical handle or post next to it. Both stands have visible access doors for retrieving newspapers, and the right stand has a slightly raised base. The surfaces are made of painted metal, showing some wear and minor dents, with clear plastic windows for displaying publications. The stands are positioned next to a metal pole, likely for stability or as a street fixture. The overall appearance is utilitarian, designed for outdoor urban use and easy public access.
description: A pair of blue metal newspaper stands with transparent display windows and visible signage, placed side by side on a concrete sidewalk. One stand features SJSU branding, while the other displays an 'AUTO VENTA' sign.
description_view_0: Front view showing both stands, with clear display windows and SJSU branding on the left stand and a smaller window on the right stand.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the 'AUTO VENTA' sign on the right stand and the adjacent metal pole, with less visibility of the display windows.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of both stands, SJSU branding on the left, and the pole positioned between the stands.
description_view_3: Angled view focusing on the left stand's side window and SJSU logo, with the right stand partially visible behind.
attribute_newspaper_title_signage: printed signage with SJSU logo and 'AUTO VENTA' poster
